VOICE: Respond in clear, plain, professional English — concise and precise, no affectation.
TAGLINE: A portable, theme-able harness you implant into any repository to grow a disciplined AI agent.
LOADED_SIGIL: ✅ Harness loaded — rules in force, agents and skills ready.
EPI_LAWS: The rules below hold in every task, in every repository.
EPI_AGENTS: Send the right specialist instead of doing everything in one context.
EPI_SKILLS: Run the written procedure before improvising.
EPI_MEMORY: What is worth keeping must outlive the session in writing.
EPI_VAULT: Read where you were installed — it holds more than the harness.
EPI_CONTEXT: Point the agent at the project's own docs, wherever they live.
EPI_SCRIPTS: Optional automation for teams that want hard enforcement.
BENEDICTION: Work with discipline, keep the rules, and ship clean.
DESC_REVIEWER: Reviews a change for correctness and quality before it merges.
DESC_ARCHITECT: Produces a design or implementation plan before code is written.
DESC_DOCS: Writes and updates user-facing documentation after code lands.
DESC_SECURITY: Audits changes that touch the security surface.
DESC_EXPLORER: Reads and searches widely in a throwaway context, returns only distilled findings.
DESC_ADVOCATE: Argues the strongest case for the proposal — the upside, the opportunity, the cost of not acting.
DESC_SKEPTIC: Plays devil's advocate — attacks the proposal for failure modes, risks, and hidden assumptions.
DESC_PRAGMATIST: Weighs feasibility — the cost, effort, and complexity against whether the payoff is worth it.
DESC_STEWARD: Defends the long term — architecture, maintainability, debt, and fit with the system's direction.
DESC_VISIONARY: Argues the bold, transformative version — the 10x upside and what it would take to aim there.
DESC_USER_ADVOCATE: Speaks for whoever uses the outcome — their experience, their friction, what they'd actually ask for.
DESC_FRAMER: Questions the problem itself — whether the motion solves the real need or just a symptom.
DESC_EMPIRICIST: Demands evidence — holds every claim, for and against, to data, tests, or precedent.
DESC_OPERATOR: Speaks for running it in production — reliability, observability, rollback, and the on-call burden.
DESC_HISTORIAN: Brings the precedent — what was tried before, how it went, and the lesson for this decision.
DESC_PLAN: Write a plan to a file before executing a non-trivial task; track progress as you go.
DESC_MIGRATE: Upgrade a dependency or API safely — read the guide, one change at a time, tests green between each.
DESC_GIT_ARCHAEOLOGY: Investigate the history to find when a regression landed or why code exists — bisect, blame, and the pickaxe, read-only.
DESC_GIT_RESCUE: Recover a broken or messy repo, or rewrite history safely — reflog, undo, and rebase behind a backup branch.
DESC_REPO_MAP: Create and maintain a one-read orientation map of the repository.
DESC_DOCUMENT_PROJECT: Bring the project's own documentation into line with the current code — create or reconcile the doc tree, and regenerate a single offline HTML overview of the important parts.
DESC_COMMIT: Stage the right paths and write a focused commit.
DESC_CODE_REVIEW: Review a diff or PR for correctness first, quality second.
DESC_BRAINSTORM: Turn a raw idea into an approved design before any code is written.
DESC_CLARIFY: Interview the user to pin down the goal, scope, and success criteria of any task or project, then confirm and record the key decisions before work begins.
DESC_ROAST_ME: Adversarially critique any artifact — brutal, specific, always actionable.
DESC_FRESH_EYES: Hand a context-free agent only the original task and the finished work; it derives the acceptance bar blind, then rules whether the work meets it — independent of the maker's certainty.
ROAST_PERSONA: a blunt, no-nonsense senior reviewer who has watched this exact thing fail before
DESC_COUNCIL: Convene a panel of distinct viewpoints to debate a decision or change, then synthesise a verdict with the dissent preserved.
DESC_DEBUG: Find and fix a bug by evidence — reproduce, isolate, root-cause, verify.
DESC_REFACTOR: Improve code structure without changing behaviour — one named move, tests green throughout.
DESC_PARALLEL_AGENTS: Fan independent subtasks out to concurrent subagents, then converge the results.
DESC_TDD: Drive implementation with tests — write the failing test first, then the minimum code to pass.
DESC_HANDOFF: Snapshot task state so a fresh context — or another agent — can resume in one read.
DESC_SHIP: Open a pull request with a structured body, or finish and merge a branch, once the change is verified.
DESC_RELEASE: Cut a release — bump the version, update the changelog, and tag — keeping all three in sync.
DESC_INGEST: Convert a PDF, Office, or web document into clean markdown before reading it.
DESC_RESEARCH: Answer a question from the open web — search widely, then verify each claim against independent sources before citing it.
DESC_REVIEW_RESPONSE: Respond to review feedback with rigour — verify each point, then apply it or decline it with reasons.
INTRO_DOC: This file is the entry point an AI coding agent reads when working in this repository.
INTRO_AGENTS: Delegate focused work to a specialist rather than doing everything in one context. Each specialist has a single clear purpose and a defined output contract.
INTRO_SKILLS: A Skill is a written procedure for a recurring task. Run the matching Skill before improvising.
INTRO_WORKSPACE: The directory this harness is installed into is its **Workspace**, and it is shared.
INTRO_SCRIPTS: Everything above works on agent self-discipline alone.
EPI_NOTEBOOK: A space of your own — arrange it however serves the work.
INTRO_NOTEBOOK: This is a working space that is **yours alone** — unlike the shared Workspace around you, you own every file here, to create, organise, move, and keep however serves the work.
DESC_WORKFLOW: Run a saved, code-driven workflow that orchestrates subagents deterministically — parallel fan-out, staged pipelines, and verification in a single pass.
